data.narrative:
Axelspace is a Series D-stage space and aerospace company founded by Yuya Nakamura. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 private companies in the same sector and stage proximity, supplemented by reference to 5 publicly traded Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Across the 9 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers, the median valuation is $1.8B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $4.2B. Notable peers in this set include Astranis at $3.5B (Series D), Relativity Space at $4.2B (Series E), ICEYE at $2.8B (Series E), and Axiom Space at $2.5B (Series C). Public-comp anchoring via EV/Revenue multiples is not applicable here, as Axelspace's revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S of 45.3x across public comps cannot be deployed without the target's top-line figure.

The triangulated range supported by available data is $1.5B to $2.1B, centered on the $1.8B peer median. Key uncertainties include the absence of Axelspace's financial metrics (revenue, burn, path to unit economics), which prevents revenue-multiple validation, and limited visibility into product maturity relative to peer cohorts. The wide peer range ($1.0B–$4.2B) also reflects meaningful dispersion in stage and business model within the Space & Aerospace sector.
